title: Nation-Leading Safety and Information Standards for Lithium-Ion Battery Products Now in Effect
content: NSW has introduced Australiaâ€™s first safety and information standards for lithium-ion battery-powered e-micromobility vehicles, aiming to reduce fire risks from poor-quality components and misuse. As of February 2025, all e-micromobility devices sold in NSW must meet new safety standards, with compliance education underway and stricter regulations taking effect in 2026. A new information standard, effective from February 19, 2025, will ensure consumers receive safety guidance at the point of sale, with penalties for non-compliance starting in August. NSW is leading a national effort to standardize regulations across Australia.
author: Analysts Account
